doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_equal_p: equality predicate. Returns true iff a == b. NaN-vs-anything returns false (matching IEEE 754; even equal_p(NaN, NaN) is false). +0 == -0 returns true (signed zero not distinguished for cmp/equal_p). Class 'arithmetic' (50ms budget).
divergence_from_c: C returns int; TS returns boolean. Predicates do NOT throw on NaN — returns false. See ../mpfr_less_p/spec.json for shared rationale. equal_p deserves a specific callout: NaN vs NaN returns false (IEEE 754 'NaN is unequal to itself') and +0 vs -0 returns true (MPFR cmp does not order signed zero) — both behaviours are inherited verbatim from the C reference.
refs: mpfr/src/cmp.c L32–L98 — and specifically L57–L58 for the +0/-0 → 0 rule that makes equal_p(+0, -0) return true.
mpfr/tests/tcomparisons.c L87–L117 — `eq_tests` exercises mpfr_equal_p on same-value-different-prec pairs.
CLAUDE.md "Hallucination-risk callouts: NaN ≠ NaN" — equal_p preserves IEEE-754 NaN-inequality; "Signed zero is real" applies to ARITHMETIC ops, NOT to cmp/equal_p.
